name: QNI : Observed File Hash Associated with Malware Threat
notes:
This rule triggers when flow content includes a file hash that matches known bad file hashes included in a Threat Intelligence data feed. Indicates that someone has transferred malware over the network.

Note: The Malware Hashes SHA reference set and the Malware Hashes MD5 reference set must be populated. The Threat Intelligence App can be used to import threat intel feeds in these Reference Sets.
conditions_text: when the flow context is Remote to Remote
when any of File Hash (QNI) are contained in any of Malware Hashes SHA - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case), Malware Hashes MD5 - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY QNI : Observed File Hash Associated with Malware Threat on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND NOT when the flow context is Remote to Remote
AND when any of File Hash (QNI) are contained in any of Malware Hashes SHA - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case), Malware Hashes MD5 - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list: Event name: QNI : Observed File Hash Associated with Malware Threat

Event description: Detects when flow content includes a file hash that matches known bad file hashes included in a Threat Intelligence data feed. Indicates that someone has transferred malware over the network.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
